Why BMIIL

The right place
at the right time

India's healthcare innovation is concentrated in 5 cities. Tier-2 India — home to 800 million people — has no medtech infrastructure. BMIIL is built to change that, starting from Bareilly with a model designed to replicate across North India.

Read More
🏥

Our Edge

Six reasons to build
your startup here

01

Tier-2 India Advantage

Lower burn, massive patient access, authentic problem statements from an underserved healthcare market that investors are increasingly watching.

02

Captive Clinical Pipeline

Direct access to BIU's teaching hospital gives startups immediate problem identification, validation partners, and pilot environments.

03

Regulatory Navigation

In-house mentors experienced in CDSCO, FDA, AYUSH, and WHO pathways — reducing time-to-compliance by months.

04

The Ayurveda Angle

No other medtech incubator in India offers a credible, evidence-based Ayurveda + technology fusion track backed by university research.

05

Grant Ecosystem Access

Active pipeline to DST, BIRAC, Startup India, and international grants — our team actively facilitates applications and submissions.

06

Multidisciplinary Faculty

200+ researchers across Medicine, Pharmacy, Biotech, Dental and Allied Health available as subject matter experts and co-innovators.

Our Story & Mission

We exist because
800 million people
deserve better.

India's medtech innovation ecosystem is clustered in 5 cities and serves a fraction of the country. BMIIL was founded on a single conviction: that the next wave of healthcare breakthroughs won't come from a metro co-working space — they'll come from people who live with the problem every day.

BMIIL
"The most important medical innovations of the next decade will be built by clinicians and engineers who understand what it means to live in Tier-2 India — not just visit it."
— BMIIL Founding Principle

Incubate

Turn clinical insights and research into validated, fundable healthcare companies

Validate

Give startups the clinical access, lab infrastructure, and regulatory expertise to prove their solutions work

Scale

Connect innovators to investors, hospital networks, and global markets — from Bareilly to the world

Our Story

The gap that
demanded
a response.

Uttar Pradesh is home to over 240 million people — more than Brazil. It has one of the highest burdens of preventable disease in South Asia. And until BMIIL, it had no dedicated medtech incubation infrastructure of any kind.

The founding idea was simple and urgent: Bareilly International University already has the hospitals, the labs, the students, and the faculty. What it lacked was a structured system to convert clinical observations into startups.

"Every day in a public hospital, doctors encounter problems that they know could be solved — if someone would just build the right tool. We are that someone."

BMIIL was built to be that bridge. Not a generic startup hub with a healthcare vertical — but a deeply clinical institution where the problem always comes before the product, and the patient always comes before the pitch.

Situated within BIU's campus in Bareilly, we have direct access to a multi-speciality teaching hospital, specialised research laboratories, and a multidisciplinary faculty — giving our startups infrastructure that most metro incubators cannot offer at any price.

Journey So Far

✓
2024 — Foundation
The Idea Takes Shape

Dr. Varun Agarwal and Claudius Lazarus identify the gap in Tier-2 India's healthcare innovation infrastructure. Initial conversations with Bareilly International University begin.

✓
2025 — Establishment
BMIIL is Founded

Entity incorporated. Hosting agreement signed with BIU. First grant applications submitted. Core team begins forming around the founding vision.

✓
2025–26 — Validation
Institutional Backing Secured

First grant approvals received. Letters of intent signed with key partners. Mentor network assembled. Infrastructure setup underway on BIU campus.

→
2026 — Launch
Cohort 1 Opens

First cohort of 10–12 startups across Medtech Incubation, Accelerator, and Ayush tracks. Demo Day and first investor introductions planned for end of year.

→
2027 — Scale
Replication Begins

Second cohort launches. Framework for replicating the BMIIL model with partner universities across North India.

What We Stand For

The principles that
guide every decision.

01

Problem First, Always

We don't incubate technology — we incubate solutions to real clinical problems. Every startup that joins BMIIL must begin with a deeply understood, patient-validated problem statement. No exceptions.

02

Rigor Over Hype

Healthcare is not a place for shortcuts. We demand clinical evidence, regulatory thinking, and intellectual honesty from every team we work with — and we model the same ourselves.

03

Tier-2 as a Strength

We don't apologise for being in Bareilly. We celebrate it. Lower costs, authentic problem exposure, and a healthcare market underserved for too long — this is an advantage, not a compromise.

04

Traditional Knowledge, Modern Evidence

India's Ayurvedic heritage is a globally unique innovation asset. We treat it with scientific seriousness — validating, protecting, and building on it rather than dismissing or romanticising it.

05

Founders Come First

Our terms are founder-friendly. Our incentive is your success. We measure ourselves by the outcomes of our portfolio companies, not by the number of startups we admit.

06

Build to Last

We are not building a programme. We are building an institution — one designed to outlast any individual, any cohort, and any funding cycle.

The Bigger Picture

BMIIL isn't just
a centre — it's a
replicable model.

India has 800+ universities outside its metro cities. The majority sit adjacent to district hospitals, have pharmacy and biotech facilities, and are surrounded by communities with unmet healthcare needs.

BMIIL's Bareilly unit is designed to prove — rigorously and publicly — that a clinically-grounded medtech incubator can be built, funded, and sustained in Tier-2 India.

The long-term vision is a national network of BMIIL-model centres, each anchored to a university with clinical infrastructure, each generating a pipeline of locally relevant healthcare startups.

2026

Bareilly — Proof of Concept

First cohort. First validated startups. First exits or funding rounds.

2027

North India Expansion

Two additional BMIIL centres in partner universities across UP and Uttarakhand.

2028

National Medtech Network

A federated network of 5+ BMIIL-model centres contributing to a shared national deal flow.

2030

Global Medtech Hub

BMIIL-model centres contributing to India's position as a global medtech innovation hub.

800M+underserved patients

In Tier-2 and Tier-3 India — the largest untapped healthcare market in the world.

₹9L Crhealthcare market

India's total healthcare market, majority concentrated in 8 metros today.

10–12startups per cohort

Selected for depth of impact, clinical rigor, and team quality — not volume.

3incubation tracks

Medtech, Accelerator, and Ayush — covering the full spectrum of healthcare innovation.

12 monthsprogramme duration

Structured phases from problem validation through regulatory readiness to investor introduction.

Growth Stage · 6 Months

Built to scale.
Built to close.

The BMIIL Accelerator is for health and wellness startups that already have a product, early customers, or traction — and need structured, intensive support to reach the next order of magnitude.

6
Month Sprint
8
Seats Available

Demo Days
3

Is This For You?

✓ This track is built for you if…

✓You have a health or wellness product that real customers have used
✓You understand your ICP and have validated the core value proposition
✓You're looking to raise your first institutional round — angel, pre-seed, or seed
✓You want to access hospital systems, pharma companies, or healthcare corporates as B2B partners
✓You need structured support with GTM, revenue operations, or channel partnerships

✗ Consider a different track if…

✗You're still building your first prototype — the Medtech Incubation track will serve you better
✗Your product is primarily an Ayurveda or traditional medicine innovation
✗You haven't yet identified a clear monetisation model or target customer segment
✗Your primary challenge is technical development, not growth or fundraising

Sprint Structure

Six months. Three focused sprints.

Sprint 1 · Month 1
Diagnosis & Positioning

An honest, structured audit of where you actually are. We stress-test your product, market understanding, team, and financials before building any growth plan.

→ Business model audit & P&L review
→ ICP validation & customer segmentation
→ Competitive positioning map
→ Fundraising readiness scorecard
→ 6-month OKR framework defined
Sprint 2 · Months 2–4
Growth & GTM Execution

Full execution sprint. Three months of structured growth activities — go-to-market, distribution, pilot partnerships, revenue operations, and investor pipeline.

→ GTM campaigns live
→ 3+ hospital or corporate pilots
→ Revenue ops system built
→ Investor pipeline of 20+
→ Mid-program demo day
Sprint 3 · Months 5–6
Fundraise & Scale

The final sprint is built around one outcome — closing capital. Investor deck refined, data room ready, term sheets negotiated.

→ Investor deck (final)
→ Data room complete
→ Demo Day pitch
→ Term sheet negotiations
→ Post-cohort investor introductions

Ready to apply for the Accelerator?

Applications are rolling. We spend more time with early applicants.

Apply for This Track →

Unique Differentiator · 12 Months

Ancient intelligence.
Modern evidence.

The only incubation track in North India dedicated to turning Ayurveda, herbal medicine, and traditional wellness knowledge into scientifically validated, commercially viable, globally competitive products — with rigorous evidence at the core.

$200B+
Global Herbal Market by 2030
₹75,000 Cr
India's Ayush Sector
<1%
Ayurvedic Products Clinically Validated
9,000+
Medicinal Plants Documented in India

The Opportunity

A global market.
Almost no credible pipeline.

The global market for herbal medicines, nutraceuticals, and traditional wellness products is approaching $200 billion. The demand has never been higher — in Europe, the US, Southeast Asia, and increasingly within India itself.

India has a head start no other country can replicate: 5,000 years of documented Ayurvedic knowledge, over 9,000 identified medicinal plants, and a Ministry of AYUSH with growing funding.

"Less than 1% of Ayurvedic formulations in the market today have been validated through modern clinical standards. That's not a reason to dismiss Ayurveda — it's the size of the opportunity."

$200B+
Global Herbal Market

Growing at 8–12% CAGR, driven by consumer demand for natural, evidence-backed wellness products

₹75,000 Cr
India's Ayush Sector

Target of ₹1.2 lakh crore by 2025 with government pushing hard on export promotion

60+
Countries with TM Policy

Regulatory pathways for traditional medicine products are opening globally

<1%
Products Validated

The gap between traditional knowledge and clinical evidence is the size of the opportunity

Is This For You?

✓ This track is built for you if…

✓You're an Ayurveda practitioner (BAMS/MD) or traditional medicine researcher
✓You want to validate traditional formulations or develop herbal nutraceuticals
✓You believe in the scientific rigor of evidence-based traditional medicine
✓You want to access AYUSH regulatory pathways and global markets
✓You have a formulation, compound, or traditional practice worth validating scientifically

✗ Consider a different track if…

✗You want to sell traditional products without clinical validation
✗Your primary innovation is a medical device or digital health tool — apply for Medtech
✗You already have a validated product with customers — consider the Accelerator
✗You're not open to subjecting traditional knowledge to modern scientific scrutiny

Ready to apply for the Ayush track?

8 seats. Rolling applications. Ancient knowledge deserves modern validation.

Apply for This Track →

Core Track · 12 Months

From clinical insight
to validated product.

Our flagship program for healthcare, biotech, and medical device innovators. If you've identified a real clinical problem and are serious about solving it — with rigour, regulatory awareness, and a plan to reach patients — this is your track.

12
Month Program
10
Seats Available
4
Structured Phases
500+
Bed Hospital Access

Is This For You?

✓ This track is built for you if…

✓You're a clinician, researcher, or engineer who has identified a specific, patient-validated clinical problem
✓You want to build a medical device, diagnostic tool, digital health solution, or biotech product
✓You're at idea, early research, or pre-prototype stage and need infrastructure to develop further
✓You want regulatory guidance (CDSCO, FDA) built into your development process from day one
✓You're willing to be physically present on the BIU campus for core program milestones

✗ Consider a different track if…

✗You already have a working product with early revenue — the Accelerator is a better fit
✗Your innovation is primarily in Ayurveda or traditional medicine — apply for the Ayush track
✗You're looking for a co-working space or a short-term mentorship programme
✗You haven't yet spent meaningful time with the clinical problem and the patients it affects

Curriculum

12 months. Four phases. Zero shortcuts.

The program is structured in four sequential phases, each with defined deliverables. You don't move to the next phase until the current one is complete and reviewed.

1
Months 1–2
Discovery & Problem Validation

Clinical immersion, problem statement refinement, initial market assessment. You'll spend the first two months in BIU's hospital wards and OPDs.

→ Validated Problem Statement
→ Stakeholder Interview Report
→ Competitive Landscape Map
→ Regulatory Pathway Scoping
2
Months 3–7
Build & Prototype

The longest phase. Full access to BIU's biotech, pharma, and medical device labs. You'll build, test, fail, and iterate — with technical advisors at every step.

→ Working Prototype / MVP
→ Lab Test Reports
→ IP Assessment
→ Technical Specification
3
Months 8–10
Validate & Regulate

Clinical validation with real patients through BIU's hospital. Regulatory filing preparation begins. IP protection strategy finalised.

→ Clinical Validation Report
→ CDSCO / FDA Filing Draft
